sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INTEGER, test_date DATE, test_list LIST<INTEGER>);
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (1, DATE '2023-01-01', [1, NULL, 3]), (2, DATE '2030-02-01', [NULL, 5, 8]);
SELECT id, test_date + INTERVAL list_any_value(test_list) DAY AS adjusted_date FROM test_dates WHERE test_date IS NOT NULL;

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id, list_any_value(test_list) AS any_value, test_date + INTERVAL any_value DAY AS effective_date FROM test_dates GROUP BY id, test_date HAVING any_value IS NOT NULL) AS derived_table;
SELECT id, SUM(CASE WHEN test_date + INTERVAL list_any_value(test_list) DAY > CURRENT_DATE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS future_dates FROM test_dates GROUP BY id HAVING SUM(test_list) IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE subquery_test (user_id INTEGER, created_at DATE, activity_list LIST<INTEGER>);
INSERT INTO subquery_test VALUES (1, DATE '2020-01-01', [NULL, 2]), (2, DATE '2019-12-31', [1, 3, NULL]);
SELECT user_id, list_any_value(activity_list) AS first_activity, created_at + INTERVAL list_any_value(activity_list) DAY AS new_date FROM subquery_test WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL;

SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT user_id, created_at + INTERVAL list_any_value(activity_list) DAY AS activity_date FROM subquery_test GROUP BY user_id, created_at ORDER BY user_id) AS activity_dates WHERE activity_date < CURRENT_DATE GROUP BY user_id;
